chunk_summary: This section outlines the weekly outpatient CMAM visit checklist and discharge criteria for managing uncomplicated wasting in children, emphasizing anthropometry, illness screening, appetite assessment, feeding plans, and discharge rules.
learning_objectives: Understand the weekly outpatient CMAM visit protocol for uncomplicated wasting.
Learn the key steps for anthropometry, illness screening, and appetite assessment.
Identify discharge criteria for children recovering from uncomplicated wasting.
Recognize the importance of feeding plans and risk management in CMAM programs.
key_facts: Anthropometry includes weight, edema check, and MUAC (6–59 months), with re-measurement if values are discordant with clinical appearance.
Appetite failure should be treated as a diagnosis with four common causes: active infection, caregiver preparation barrier, sharing/diversion, and low caregiver–child interaction quality.
Feeding plans should include 150–185 kcal/kg/day of RUTF until recovery, with adjustments for sharing risks.
Discharge criteria: normalization of both WHZ ≥ -2 and MUAC ≥ 125 mm, with follow-up if only one normalizes.
Escalation is required if weight loss occurs for two consecutive visits or if edema persists.
reference_quote: Teach an explicit exit rule: recovery should ideally normalize both WHZ ≥ -2 and MUAC ≥ 125 mm before discharge, with planned follow-up if only one normalizes.
